body.content:
Kyoto Day Trips - Inside Kyoto
Visiting Kyoto but want a trip outside the city? Here is our list of the best towns, temples, hikes, and other attractions that can be visited in a day.
![Kifune-jinja Shrine](https://photos.smugmug.com/photos/i-DbVkNDF/0/L/i-DbVkNDF-L.jpg "Kifune-jinja Shrine")
Kifune-jinja Shrine
### The List: Day Trips From Kyoto
Seven days in Kyoto gives plenty of time to fit in at least one day trip outside the city centre. This is our list of the best places within reasonable distance to make a day of it. See our [Kyoto Itineraries](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-itineraries) for more suggestions on how to make the most of your time. We’ve also listed temples and shrines which require a half or full day to visit, and then finish off with some spectacular day hikes, as well as some shorter and longer alternatives. Click through for full details on what to expect.
![One of the thatched-roof houses in Kayabuki-no-Sato – image © Chris Rowthorn](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "One of the thatched-roof houses in Kayabuki-no-Sato – image © Chris Rowthorn")
One of the thatched-roof houses in Kayabuki-no-Sato – image © Chris Rowthorn
### Kyoto Day Trip Destinations
##### [Uji](https://www.insidekyoto.com/uji)
About 15 kms southwest of Kyoto, and easily accessed by both the Keihan Line and the JR Line, Uji is famous for the lovely Byodo-in Temple (the temple on the back of the Y10 coin). It’s also famous as a tea growing area.
![Byodoin Temple Buddhist temple](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Byodoin Temple Buddhist temple")
Byodoin Temple Buddhist temple
##### [A Day Trip To Ohara](https://www.insidekyoto.com/a-day-trip-to-ohara)
Ohara is an ancient farming village north of Kyoto, famous for its rural beauty and the historical and spiritual significance of its many temples. Michael Lambe takes us on a comprehensive tour of this area’s key sites.
![The village of Ohara – image © Michael Lambe](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The village of Ohara – image © Michael Lambe")
The village of Ohara – image © Michael Lambe
##### [Himeji Day Trip Itinerary](https://www.insidekyoto.com/himeji-day-trip-itinerary)
If you’re a fan of Japanese castles, you must visit Himeji, which is only 45 minutes from Kyoto Station by bullet train. Make the most of your time with our Himeji Day Trip Itinerary.
![Himeji Castle on a clear day. – image © Florentyna Leow](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Himeji Castle on a clear day. – image © Florentyna Leow")
Himeji Castle on a clear day. – image © Florentyna Leow
##### [Nara Travel Guide](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nara-travel-guide)
Nara is a spectacularly picturesque city which is second only to Kyoto for the richness and beauty of its temples, shrines and gardens. It’s possible to visit Nara as a daytrip from Kyoto and Osaka, but its gorgeous districts and parks are certainly worth a few days of your time.
![Cherry blossoms at Todai-ji Temple © fortherock](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='531'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20531'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Cherry blossoms at Todai-ji Temple © fortherock")
Cherry blossoms at Todai-ji Temple © [fortherock](https://www.flickr.com/photos/fortherock/7140984161)
##### [Kurama And Kibune](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-and-kibune)
Kurama and Kibune are a pair of tranquil rural villages and easy and scenic 30-minute train north of Kyoto on Eizan Line. They form the best half-day trip out of Kyoto.
![Kurama dera Temple, Honden Kondo](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kurama dera Temple, Honden Kondo")
Kurama dera Temple, Honden Kondo
##### [The Kitayama Area: Bujo-ji, Miyama, Kayabuki-no-Sato](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kitayama-area-bujo-ji-miyama-kayabuki-no-sato)
If you want to experience traditional rural Japan and you’re willing to drive a rental car, the Kitayama Mountains north of Kyoto make the perfect day trip.
![Kayabuki-no-Sato thatched roof house village in Miyama © Pang Yu Liu](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kayabuki-no-Sato thatched roof house village in Miyama © Pang Yu Liu")
Kayabuki-no-Sato thatched roof house village in Miyama © [Pang Yu Liu](https://www.flickr.com/photos/pangyuliu/36108171293)
##### [Hiroshima And Miyajima Travel Guide](https://www.insidekyoto.com/hiroshima-and-miyajima-travel-guide)
Less than two hours by bullet train from Kyoto, Hiroshima is the most interesting city in Western Japan. And nearby Miyajima Island is a natural and spiritual sanctuary.
![Hanami party near Hiroshima Peace Park © 16782093@N03](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Hanami party near Hiroshima Peace Park © 16782093@N03")
Hanami party near Hiroshima Peace Park © [16782093@N03](https://www.flickr.com/photos/16782093@N03/6762192793)
### Kyoto Temples and Shrines Outside The City
##### [Kurama-dera Mountain Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-dera-mountain-temple)
Kurama-dera Mountain Temple is easily the best day- or half-day trip out of Kyoto. High on the shoulder of Mt. Kurama, you can feel the power of nature up here.
![Kurama-dera Mountain Temple](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kurama-dera Mountain Temple")
Kurama-dera Mountain Temple
##### [Sanzen-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/sanzen-in-temple)
Sanzen-in Temple, located in the village of Ohara (see earlier entry), is one of the most beautiful temples in the mountains outside the city.
![Sanzen-in Temple](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Sanzen-in Temple")
Sanzen-in Temple
##### [Byodo-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/byodo-in-temple)
A superb Hiean-era temple in the southeast suburb of Uji, Byodo-in a brilliant place to visit for those who want to step off the beaten track.
![Byodo-in temple](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Byodo-in temple")
Byodo-in temple
##### [Kamigamo-jinja (Kamowakeikazuchi-jinja) Shrine](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kamigamo-jinja-kamowakeikazuchi-jinja-shrine)
One of Kyoto’s least visited but most historically important shrines, Kamigamo-jinja Shrine is a great place to catch your thoughts up north.
![Kamigamo Shrine](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='550'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20550'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kamigamo Shrine")
Kamigamo Shrine
### Kyoto Day Hikes
##### [Kurama to Kibune Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kurama-to-kibune-hike)
The easy hike from Kurama to Kibune, in the hills north of Kyoto, is our favorite half-day trip out of Kyoto City. It’s the perfect combination of nature and culture.
![Kurama-dera Main Hall with Cherries – image © Chris Rowthorn](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='450'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20450'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kurama-dera Main Hall with Cherries – image © Chris Rowthorn")
Kurama-dera Main Hall with Cherries – image © Chris Rowthorn
##### [Fushimi-Inari Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto)
The hike to the summit of 233m Mt. Inari-san and the pilgrimage circle around the shrines near the top is one of the most interesting short walks around Kyoto. It’s also the best way to see all of Fushima-Inari Taisha Shrine.
![Fushimi-Inari Shrine © Damien Douxchamps](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='650'%20height='435'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20435'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Fushimi-Inari Shrine © Damien Douxchamps")
Fushimi-Inari Shrine © [Damien Douxchamps](https://damien.douxchamps.net/media/photo/_0061777/)
##### [Walking the Nakasendo from Kyoto Guide and Map](https://www.insidekyoto.com/walking-the-nakasendo-from-kyoto-guide-and-map)
Walking the Nakasendo, an old mountain route between Kyoto and Tokyo, is a great way to sample Japan’s rural scenery. You can walk the most popular section, Magome to Tsumago, as a daytrip from Kyoto.
![Magome-juku in late afternoon © James Faulkner](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Magome-juku in late afternoon © James Faulkner")
Magome-juku in late afternoon © [James Faulkner](https://www.flickr.com/photos/captainfaulkers/38655045435)
##### [Takao to Hozukyo Hike via Kiyotaki and Kuya-no-taki Waterfall](https://www.insidekyoto.com/takao-hozukyo-hike-via-kiyotaki-kuya-no-taki-waterfall)
The hike from the hamlet of Takao down through the mountains west of Kyoto to the train station at Hozukyo is one of the best day hikes near Kyoto. It includes two superb temples, a crystal-clear river and a magical waterfall.
![Lunch Spot on River – image © Chris Rowthorn](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='450'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20450'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Lunch Spot on River – image © Chris Rowthorn")
Lunch Spot on River – image © Chris Rowthorn
### Shorter Kyoto Hikes
##### [Mt Daimonji-yama Hike from Ginkaku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/mt-daimonji-yama-hike-ginkaku-ji-temple)
The one-hour roundtrip hike from Ginkaku-ji Temple up to the viewpoint on Mt. Daimonji-yama is the best short hike in Kyoto. The view of the city from here is incredible! Found out everything you need to do this hike.
![The view over Kyoto from the Daimonji viewpoint – image © Chris Rowthorn](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='450'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20450'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The view over Kyoto from the Daimonji viewpoint – image © Chris Rowthorn")
The view over Kyoto from the Daimonji viewpoint – image © Chris Rowthorn
##### [Shogunzuka and Seiryuden Hike From Chion-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shogunzuka-seiryuden-hike-chion-temple)
One of the best short hikes in Kyoto is the easy 2-hour hike from Chion-in Temple in Southern Higashiyama up to the Shogunzuka viewpoint. It provides some incredible views over Kyoto. Here is everything you need to do this hike.
![The view over Kyoto from the Seiryuden viewpoint – image © Jeffrey Friedl](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='533'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20533'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The view over Kyoto from the Seiryuden viewpoint – image © Jeffrey Friedl")
The view over Kyoto from the Seiryuden viewpoint – image © Jeffrey Friedl
### Overnight Kyoto Trips
While not really daytrip distance from the city, these two areas are well worth an overnight trip from Kyoto.
##### [The Kumano Kodo Walking Trail: A Guide with Maps](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kumano-kodo-walking-trail-guide-with-maps)
The Kumano Kodo is an ancient pilgrimage trail in the mountains of Wakayama (south of Kyoto) where you can walk with a light pack from guesthouse to guesthouse for a few days. It’s a brilliant way to experience Japan’s rural side and ancient religious traditions.
![Kumano Kodo near Tsugizakura-oji – image © Courtesy of the Tanabe City Kumano Tourism Bureau](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='600'%20height='394'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20394'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kumano Kodo near Tsugizakura-oji – image © Courtesy of the Tanabe City Kumano Tourism Bureau")
Kumano Kodo near Tsugizakura-oji – image © Courtesy of the Tanabe City Kumano Tourism Bureau
##### [Exploring the Tango-hanto Peninsula](https://www.insidekyoto.com/tango-hanto-peninsula)
The Tango-hanto Peninsula, about two hours north of Kyoto City, is a beautiful unspoiled destination with onsen (hot springs), beaches and stunning coastal scenery. It’s the perfect place to enjoy rural Japan without the crowds. Here is our full Tango guide and map.
![Tango coastline – image © Hamish Pidgeon](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='444'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20444'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Tango coastline – image © Hamish Pidgeon")
Tango coastline – image © Hamish Pidgeon
### Kyoto Hiking Guides
##### [Best Kyoto Hikes](https://www.insidekyoto.com/best-kyoto-hikes)
Surrounded by mountains on three sides, Kyoto has some brilliant hiking. There are good hikes right in the city and others just a short train or bus trip away. Here are my five favorite hikes in and near Kyoto.
![Hiking paradise](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Hiking paradise")
Hiking paradise
##### [Kyoto Hiking Itineraries](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-hiking-itineraries)
Kyoto is surrounded by mountains on three sides and these mountains are latticed with hiking trails. Here, we introduce the five best hikes in or near Kyoto.
![The Daimonji viewpoint](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='450'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20450'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The Daimonji viewpoint")
The Daimonji viewpoint – image © Chris Rowthorn
##### [Hiking in Japan – A Full Guide](https://www.insidekyoto.com/hiking-in-japan-a-full-guide)
Japan is a fantastic hiking destination, with great mountains, well-maintained trails and an extensive mountain hut network. You can do everything from easy day hikes to multi-day treks.
![Hiking in the Japan Alps: Blue Planet Studio / Shutterstock.com](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='450'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20450'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Hiking in the Japan Alps: Blue Planet Studio / Shutterstock.com")
Hiking in the Japan Alps: [Blue Planet Studio / Shutterstock.com](https://www.shutterstock.com/g/BiancoBlue85)
### Kyoto Vacation Checklist
* [ESSENTIALS](https://www.insidekyoto.com/first-time-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-01) – For all the essentials in a brief overview, see my [First Time In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/first-time-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-02) guide
* [HOTELS](https://www.booking.com/city/jp/kyoto.html?aid=965263;label=IK-Checklist-03) – Check Kyoto accommodation availability on [Booking.com](https://www.booking.com/city/jp/kyoto.html?aid=965263;label=IK-Checklist-04) and [Agoda.com](https://www.agoda.com/partners/partnersearch.aspx?pcs=1&cid=1592349&city=1784&tag=IK-Checklist-05) - often you can book with no upfront payment and free cancellation
* [BULLET TRAIN](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826984&k_site=https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/81022-japan-rails/) – You can buy [shinkansen (bullet train) tickets online](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826984&k_site=https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/81022-japan-rails/) from Klook - popular routes include [Tokyo to Kyoto](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1305/tokyo-to-kyoto-trains/), [Kyoto to Osaka](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1474/kyoto-to-osaka-trains/) and [Kyoto to Tokyo](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1307/kyoto-to-tokyo-trains/)
* [WHERE TO STAY](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-06) – Need tips on where to stay? See my one page guide [Where To Stay In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-07)
* [PACKING LIST](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-08) – See my comprehensive [Packing List For Japan](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-09)
* [SIM CARDS](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=2652&aff_adid=877200&k_site=https://www.klook.com/search/result/?query=japan%20esim) – You can buy an [eSim to activate in Japan](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=877279&k_site=https://www.klook.com/search/result/?query=japan%20esim) or buy a data-only SIM card online for collection when you arrive at [Tokyo's Narita or Haneda Airports](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=tokyo sim&searchSource=8&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-10>) or [Kansai International Airport](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=kansai sim&searchSource=8&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-11>). You can also rent an [unlimited data pocket wifi router](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=japan wifi&searchSource=7&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-12>)
* [FLIGHTS](https://www.skyscanner.net/g/referrals/v1/flights/cheap-flights-to?adId=1100662&campaignId=13416&destination=tyoa&mediaPartnerId=1924024) – Compare Japan flight prices and timings to [find the best deals](https://www.skyscanner.net/g/referrals/v1/flights/cheap-flights-to?adId=1100662&campaignId=13416&destination=tyoa&mediaPartnerId=1924024)
* [JAPAN RAIL PASS](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=616549&k_site=https://www.klook.com/activity/1420-7-day-whole-japan-rail-pass-jr-pass/) – If you're making frequent train journeys during your visit, you might save money with [Japan Rail Pass](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=616549&k_site=https://www.klook.com/activity/1420-7-day-whole-japan-rail-pass-jr-pass/) – see [if it's worth it](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-rail-pass-is-it-worth-it?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-13) for you
* [TRAVEL INSURANCE](https://www.tkqlhce.com/click-7919142-15371527) – [World Nomads](https://www.tkqlhce.com/click-7647395-15371527) offers simple and flexible travel insurance. Buy at home or while traveling and claim online from anywhere in the world
* [WANT HELP?](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-14) – Do you want help planning your trip? Chris Rowthorn and his team of Japan experts at [Japan Travel Consulting](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-15) can help
### Kyoto District Map
Click a Kyoto District for detailed info on attractions, places to eat and accommodation
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='672'%20height='476'%20viewBox='0%200%20672%20476'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
* [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama)

* [Northern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/northern-higashiyama)

* [Kyoto Station Area](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-station-area)
* [South East Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southeast-kyoto)
![Chris Rowthorn](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='75'%20height='75'%20viewBox='0%200%2075%2075'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Chris Rowthorn")
### About InsideKyoto.com and Chris Rowthorn
* Chris Rowthorn is the co-founder of [InsideKyoto.com](https://www.insidekyoto.com) and has an unparalleled travel knowledge of Kyoto spanning three decades. Chris has written or commissioned and edited every page on the Inside Kyoto website.
* A former author of **Lonely Planet Kyoto** and **Lonely Planet Japan** guidebooks, Chris is also the director of the Kyoto walking tour company [Chris Rowthorn Tours](http://www.chrisrowthorn.com), and [Japan Travel Consulting](https://insideosaka.com/japan-travel-consulting/), where you can get bespoke travel advice for your Japan trip.
* [InsideKyoto.com](https://www.insidekyoto.com) has been cited by the **[New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/17/travel/booking-hotels-in-asia-the-getaway.html)** and **[The Times of London](https://www.thetimes.com/article/926c41d6-bb7d-11e5-8ae7-f2021079f4dd)**, among many other publications and websites.

designed to provide a means for sites to earn advertising fees by advertising and linking to amazon.com and amazon.co.uk.

We do not represent World Nomads. This is information only and not a recommendation to buy travel insurance.
